dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T are four near-identical classes (~300 lines each) with copy-paste method implementations. A base class pattern would eliminate 900+ lines of duplication.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: search.py at 1880 lines violates the 1000-line limit. The _search_query helper and _Result/_MetaResult type aliases are duplicated across search.py and engine.py.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Heavy use of Any across process.py, engine.py, and search.py parameters. Return types use unsafe union patterns.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: Duplicate assignments and copy-pasted get_top_n_reranked blend logic across all 4 BM25 variants.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: engine.py and search.py both define _search_query, _Result, and _MetaResult independently. Retriever accesses private attributes of HybridSearch.
dimension_notes.package_organization: search.py is a monolith with 4 BM25 classes, HybridSearch, Reranker, and helpers. Should be split.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: The overall architecture is sound but search.py being a 1880-line monolith with massive class duplication detracts from elegance.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Rust algorithms are well-optimized (Myers bit-parallel, SIMD-friendly). Some minor issues like redundant vector allocations in editops multiword.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: The public API surface is inconsistent across sub-modules — search.py exports Document (should be from document.py only), _Result/_MetaResult defined in 3 places.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: WRatio/QRatio use PascalCase while all other fuzz functions use snake_case. BM25Variant vs BM25Algorithm literal types for the same concept.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: The codebase has cleanly separated optional dependencies with lazy imports but some exception chains are swallowed.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: BM25 classes rebuild the entire Rust index on add_documents/remove_documents. HybridSearch.__init__ does too much in one call.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: query.py re-defines _Result and _MetaResult instead of importing from search.py, suggesting an incomplete consolidation.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Good metric-level tests but no parametrized tests verifying API parity across BM25 variants.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: The codebase shows minimal AI-generated debt. The copy-paste across BM25 variants looks more like manual duplication than AI-generated boilerplate. Code comments and docstrings are substantive and contextual.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Not directly applicable — this is a library, not a service. No auth concerns. Minor deduction for the langchain.py lazy class-building pattern which uses module-level __getattr__ that could confuse static analyzers.

issues.summary: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T are near-identical classes with copy-pasted methods — ~900 duplicated lines
evidence: BM25 (lines 71-510), BM25L (lines 513-825), BM25Plus (lines 828-1140), BM25T share get_top_n, get_top_n_fuzzy, get_top_n_rrf, fuzzy_only, explain, get_idf, get_document_vector, add_documents, remove_documents, get_top_n_reranked, get_top_n_phrase, async wrappers, and fluent builder methods with identical implementations
issues.suggestion: Extract a BaseBM25 class containing all shared methods. Each variant subclass only overrides __init__ to call the correct _rustfuzz constructor. The shared ~25 methods can be inherited.

issues.summary: search.py at 1880 lines exceeds stated 1000 line limit and contains too many responsibilities
evidence: search.py contains BM25 (71-510), BM25L (513-825), BM25Plus (828-1140), BM25T (1145-1455), HybridSearch (1458-1750), Reranker class, helper functions _enrich, _build_corpus_index, _validate_metadata, and _search_query
issues.suggestion: After extracting a BaseBM25 base class, split into bm25_base.py (base + helpers), bm25_variants.py (subclasses), hybrid.py (HybridSearch), and reranker.py.

issues.summary: BM25L.__init__ assigns self._normalize_scores twice on adjacent lines
evidence: Line 532-533: 'self._normalize_scores = normalize_scores' appears twice in BM25L.__init__
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate assignment on line 533.

issues.summary: BM25Plus.__init__ assigns self._normalize_scores twice on adjacent lines
evidence: Lines 847-848: 'self._normalize_scores = normalize_scores' appears twice in BM25Plus.__init__
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate assignment on line 848.

issues.summary: _search_query helper function is defined identically in both search.py and engine.py
evidence: search.py line 26-29 defines '_search_query(owner)' with identical lazy import of SearchQuery; engine.py line 156-160 has same definition
issues.suggestion: Define _search_query in one location (search.py or a shared module) and import it where needed.

issues.summary: _Result and _MetaResult type aliases are defined identically in search.py, engine.py, and query.py
evidence: search.py lines 21-22, engine.py lines 55-56, and query.py lines 36-37 all define _Result = tuple[str, float] and _MetaResult = tuple[str, float, Any]
issues.suggestion: Define these type aliases in a single location (e.g. a _types.py module) and import them everywhere.

issues.summary: Excessive use of Any in function signatures where more specific Protocol types could be used
evidence: process.py uses 'Callable[..., float]' for scorer but should use a Protocol; engine.py __init__ uses 'corpus: Iterable[str] | Iterable[Any] | Any' which is just Any; search.py uses 'reranker: Any' throughout
issues.suggestion: Define Protocol classes for Scorer, Reranker, and EmbeddingCallback. Use overloads for methods that return different types based on metadata.

issues.summary: Retriever accesses private _corpus, _metadata, _embeddings attributes of HybridSearch and BM25
evidence: engine.py line 548: 'self._corpus = self._hybrid._corpus'; line 549: 'self._metadata = self._hybrid._metadata'; line 779: 'emb_for_pickle = self._hybrid._embeddings'
issues.suggestion: Expose corpus, metadata as read-only properties on BM25/HybridSearch and access via public interface.

issues.summary: get_top_n_reranked blend logic (~30 lines) is copy-pasted across BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T
evidence: BM25.get_top_n_reranked (333-383), BM25L.get_top_n_reranked (661-711), BM25Plus.get_top_n_reranked (974-1024) share identical blend alpha logic
issues.suggestion: Extract the blend logic into a standalone function _blend_reranked_scores and call from each class (or base class).

issues.summary: from_column class methods have variant-specific return type annotations instead of Self
evidence: BM25.from_column returns '-> BM25', BM25L.from_column returns '-> BM25L', BM25Plus returns '-> BM25Plus'
issues.suggestion: Use typing.Self (Python 3.11+) for from_column return types.

issues.summary: BM25.explain calls self._corpus.index(doc) which raises ValueError with an unhelpful message when doc is not found
evidence: BM25.explain line 256: 'idx = self._corpus.index(doc)' raises 'ValueError: ... is not in list'
issues.suggestion: Catch the ValueError and raise a more descriptive error: 'Document not found in corpus'.

issues.summary: No parametrized test verifies API parity across BM25 variants
evidence: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T should all support the same methods but no parametrized test enforces this contract
issues.suggestion: Add a parametrized test that instantiates each variant and verifies all common methods exist and return correct types.

issues.summary: WRatio and QRatio use PascalCase while all other functions use snake_case
evidence: fuzz.py exports WRatio and QRatio alongside snake_case functions like partial_ratio, token_sort_ratio
issues.suggestion: Add snake_case aliases (w_ratio, q_ratio) alongside the existing names for consistency.

issues.summary: Scorer resolution logic (_scorer, scorer_name, scorer_obj) is repeated 4 times in process.py
evidence: Lines 34-39, 82-84, 106-108, 139-141 repeat the same scorer resolution pattern
issues.suggestion: Extract a _resolve_scorer(scorer) helper that returns (scorer_name, scorer_obj) tuple.

issues.summary: search.py is a 1880-line monolith mixing 6 distinct classes with tightly coupled internals
evidence: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T, HybridSearch, and Reranker are all in one file with shared helpers, making the module hard to navigate and maintain
issues.suggestion: Split search.py into focused modules: base class with shared helpers, variant subclasses, HybridSearch, and Reranker.  This improves navigability and encapsulation.

issues.summary: levenshtein_editops_multiword allocates new_vp and new_vn vectors inside the per-character loop
evidence: algorithms.rs line 396-397: 'let mut new_vp = vec![0u64; words]; let mut new_vn = vec![0u64; words];' allocates inside the per-character loop, unlike myers_multiword which hoists and swaps
issues.suggestion: Hoist new_vp/new_vn allocations outside the loop and use std::mem::swap, matching the pattern in myers_multiword (line 154-155).

issues.summary: Document class is re-exported from search.py via noqa comment instead of being imported from document.py
evidence: search.py line 16: 'from .document import Document  # noqa: F401 — re-export for backward compat' — Document is already properly exported from __init__.py
issues.suggestion: Remove the Document re-export from search.py since it's already available at the package level from document.py.

issues.summary: Two different Literal types exist for BM25 algorithm selection: BM25Algorithm and BM25Variant
evidence: search.py line 18 defines BM25Algorithm = Literal['bm25', 'bm25okapi', 'bm25l', 'bm25+', 'bm25plus', 'bm25t'] while join.py line 17 defines BM25Variant = Literal['BM25Okapi', 'BM25L', 'BM25Plus', 'BM25T'] with different casing and values
issues.suggestion: Consolidate into a single BM25Algorithm type using consistent casing and export from a single location.

issues.summary: BM25 algorithm names use mixed casing conventions: lowercase in search.py, PascalCase in join.py
evidence: search.py: 'bm25', 'bm25okapi', 'bm25l', 'bm25+', 'bm25plus', 'bm25t' (lowercase); join.py: 'BM25Okapi', 'BM25L', 'BM25Plus', 'BM25T' (PascalCase)
issues.suggestion: Standardize on one convention (lowercase preferred since it matches the Literal usage pattern in search.py) and map internally.

issues.summary: Some lazy import error handlers lose the original exception context
evidence: engine.py line 178-181: 'except ImportError: raise ImportError(...) from None' discards original traceback, making debugging harder when embed-anything fails to import due to a dependency issue rather than missing install
issues.suggestion: Use 'from e' instead of 'from None' in raise statements to preserve the causal chain for debugging.

issues.summary: add_documents and remove_documents rebuild the entire Rust index from scratch
evidence: BM25.add_documents line 306-307 recreates BM25Index with full corpus; BM25.remove_documents line 327-328 does the same. This is O(N) per mutation.
issues.suggestion: Document the rebuild cost in docstrings. Consider batch mutation APIs or note that callers should accumulate changes and rebuild once.

issues.summary: _Result and _MetaResult defined in 3 separate files instead of imported from a single source
evidence: search.py lines 21-22, engine.py lines 55-56, query.py lines 36-37 all independently define the same type aliases
issues.suggestion: Create a _types.py module with _Result and _MetaResult, import everywhere else.

issues.summary: search.py at 1880 lines contains 6 classes and should be a sub-package
evidence: search.py contains BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T, HybridSearch, Reranker — each substantial enough for its own module
issues.suggestion: Convert to a rustfuzz/search/ package with __init__.py re-exporting public names, and separate modules for base/variants/hybrid/reranker.

issues.summary: BM25 variant classes appear to be copy-pasted boilerplate that could have been generated by AI — identical method bodies across 4 classes
evidence: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T each have ~25 identical methods with only the Rust constructor call differing — typical pattern of AI-assisted generation without subsequent refactoring
issues.suggestion: Refactor into a base class to eliminate the duplicated boilerplate, whether AI-generated or not.

issues.summary: langchain.py uses module-level __getattr__ to lazily build classes, which can confuse type checkers and import analysis
evidence: langchain.py lines 144-148: __getattr__ dynamically builds RustfuzzBM25Retriever at first access, with a duplicate TYPE_CHECKING-only class declaration on line 156 to appease static analysis
issues.suggestion: Consider using a simpler lazy import pattern or document the TYPE_CHECKING workaround more clearly.
